sections.paragraph:
δυσκόλως adv. of δύσκολος (s. δύσκολος; Isocr., Pla.+; Vett. Val. 123, 2; POxy 1294, 10; PPrinc 102, 9; Jos., Ant. 4, 87) hardly, w. difficulty εἰσέρχεσθαι εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν Mt 19:23; Mk 10:23; Hs 9, 20, 2. εἰσπορεύεσθαι Lk 18:24. ζῆν attain (eternal) life Hm 4, 3, 6; Hs 9, 23, 3. σῴζεσθαι m 9:6. ἀποθανεῖσθαι Hs 8, 10, 2; ἡμεροῦσθαι m 12, 1, 2. κολλᾶσθαί τινι Hs 9, 20, 2.—M-M. s.v. δύσκολος. Spicq.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PPrinc PPrinc = Papyri in the Princeton University Collections—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Hm Hm = Mandates

m m = Mandates

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 265
